justification: All six pass criteria met: (1) Context coupling explicitly identified as a domain model change — 'fundamentally changes the relationship between two currently independent bounded contexts.' (2) Data ownership question raised — Available Stock defined as a derived concept distinct from StockLevel, BR-6 enforces context boundaries, escalation on real-time indicator vs. context autonomy addresses who owns the read model. (3) TOCTOU/race condition identified — AC-3 tests concurrent orders for last item, BR-3 explains why raw StockLevel allows double-selling, edge case 'Concurrent orders exhaust stock simultaneously' requires serialized or optimistic-concurrency checks. (4) Stock reservation vs. check distinction surfaced — BR-3 vs BR-4 explicitly distinguish checking Available Stock from creating a reservation, and a dedicated escalation flags that reservations are 'not mentioned anywhere in the spec but are mandatory for correctness.' (5) Escalation to MJ for architectural coupling — five detailed escalations covering blocked-order semantics, reservation mechanism, partial fulfillment, real-time vs eventual consistency contradiction, and Warehouse unavailability. (6) New cross-context invariant stated — BR-2 (order blocked if insufficient stock), BR-4 (reservation must be created atomically), BR-6 (Sales must not mutate Warehouse state).

observations.text: Exceptional depth on the reservation mechanism — identified it as missing from the spec and mandatory for correctness, with three concrete options and a recommendation. This goes beyond the rubric's requirement to 'surface' the distinction.
TOCTOU is not just mentioned but operationalized: BR-3 explains the race, BR-4 prescribes the fix, AC-3 tests the concurrent scenario, and the edge case specifies serialization requirements.
The contradiction escalation (real-time indicator vs. bounded context autonomy) demonstrates genuine domain reasoning — Bird caught that the spec's own requirements conflict.
Confidence level of 45 is well-calibrated given the number of unresolved ambiguities. The low/high confidence area breakdown is precise and actionable.
Rejection reasons preemptively block all three major failure modes: raw StockLevel checks, direct cross-context DB access, and using the read model as authoritative source.
Warehouse unavailability escalation adds a production-critical concern not in the rubric but highly relevant to the integration.

Escalations reference MJ's architectural role implicitly (type: 'missing_context', 'contradiction') but never explicitly name MJ as the escalation target. The routing is correct in spirit but could be more explicit.
